commits.subject: chore(sancio): test-helper consistency, spy hygiene, prefix-coupling note
commits.body:
Four small cleanups surfaced during a fresh-eyes audit. None change
behavior; all close consistency or hygiene gaps.

(1) cron.test.ts: replace inline buildEmptyModulesQuery() with
createMockQueryBuilder() from _helpers.ts. The shared helper already
supports the bare-then/await shape these tests need (no .single()),
and this matches every other sancio test in the file. Removes a
duplicate mock-builder pattern.

(2) cron.test.ts: consolidate process.env.CRON_SECRET cleanup into a
single file-level afterEach. Previously both an inner afterEach (in
the helper-describe) and an outer afterEach ran the same delete on
every test in that block. Harmless but ugly.

(3) dispatcher.test.ts: add afterAll to mockRestore the console.error
spy. Vitest isolates test files today, so the leaked spy is harmless
in the current setup, but it's a side effect that would silently
swallow console.error in an unrelated suite if a future shared-runtime
change ever changes that isolation guarantee.

(4) api/sancio/[...slug].ts: add a one-line note above SANCIO_PREFIX
spelling out that the constant is coupled to the file's location on
disk and to vercel.json rewrites — there is no runtime check that
would catch a drift if either ever moves. Future-proofs the next
reader against silently breaking routing by relocating the dispatcher.

166/166 tests still pass; typecheck clean.

Tags: testing, refactor, hygiene, sancio
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: test-helper consistency, spy hygiene, prefix-coupling note

commits.subject: fix(sancio): close cron auth fail-open; constant-time secret comparison
commits.body:
Both cron handlers (lib/routes/sancio/cron.ts) gated on
`if (cronSecret && authHeader !== "Bearer " + cronSecret) return 401`.
When CRON_SECRET was unset, the conditional short-circuited and the
handler ran unauthenticated — every HTTP caller could trigger real
notifications (nudge) and real module expirations (auto-expire). Vercel
auto-sets the env var for production, so the practical exposure was
limited to misconfigured deployments and previews, but the gate was
silently absent on those.

Extract isAuthorizedCron(authHeader) helper. Fail-closed on every
unauthorized shape:
- CRON_SECRET unset or empty
- Authorization header missing or empty
- Token does not match
- Prefix wrong-cased ("bearer ...")
- Trailing whitespace
- Any length mismatch

Comparison via crypto.timingSafeEqual to avoid leaking the secret
through string-comparison short-circuit timing. Length guard runs
before timingSafeEqual (which throws on length mismatch); the expected
length is not itself a secret (CRON_SECRET is a fixed-length value
provisioned by Vercel and visible to anyone with project access).

Helper stays local to cron.ts. The only callers are the two cron
handlers in this file; lifting it into lib/auth.ts would conflate
unrelated auth schemes (JWT for user routes vs shared-secret for cron).

Tests (api/__tests__/sancio/cron.test.ts, new — 22 tests):
- isAuthorizedCron helper across every input shape
- Per-endpoint integration: gate rejects before any Supabase call,
  OPTIONS preflight bypasses gate (CORS), non-GET returns 405, valid
  bearer reaches handler body. Both nudge and autoExpire covered.

Docs:
- AI_MISTAKES.md: new entry recording the fail-open pattern
  (`if (secret && doesNotMatch)` is one keyword off from correct and
  inverts under unset secret; auth checks must be fail-closed by
  construction).
- API.md: cron section spells out the fail-closed behavior + constant-
  time comparison.
- USER_ACTIONS.md: drop the now-obsolete "200 = fail-open" outcome row
  from the cron verification step (the gate now fail-closes regardless
  of CRON_SECRET state).
- SESSION_NOTES.md: updated to reflect the fix and the broader scope of
  this branch (routing + cron auth).

Tags: bugfix, security, sancio, cron, testing, documentation
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: api
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: close cron auth fail-open; constant-time secret comparison

commits.subject: docs(sancio): cover the CRON_SECRET fail-open in cron-routing verification
commits.body:
Step 3's "no auth → expect 401" guidance assumed CRON_SECRET is set on
the target deployment. lib/routes/sancio/cron.ts opens with
`if (cronSecret && authHeader !== ...)` — when the env var is unset the
auth check is bypassed entirely and the handler runs with full side
effects. Vercel auto-sets CRON_SECRET for production deployments but
not necessarily for preview/branch deployments, so the previous
guidance had a hole: a user verifying on a preview without that env
var would get 200 + real notifications/expirations instead of 401.

Add a fourth outcome row covering the 200 case: stop testing, set the
env var, retry. Reader can now diagnose every response shape from the
doc alone.

Tags: documentation, sancio, cron, safety
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: cover the CRON_SECRET fail-open in cron-routing verification

commits.subject: fix(sancio): percent-decode URL segments; correct cron verification guidance
commits.body:
Two issues found on a follow-up audit of the req.url-based dispatcher:

(1) Behavioral regression vs the previous implementation. The old code
read req.query.slug, which Node's querystring parser had already
percent-decoded — so a segment captured from "/api/sancio/projects/foo%20bar"
arrived at handlers as "foo bar". The new path-splitting approach skipped
that decode, leaving handlers to receive "foo%20bar" verbatim.

For the current Sancio routes (UUIDs + literal action words like "status",
"split") there is no observable difference, but the semantics change is
silent and would bite the moment any segment ever held a space, slash,
or other reserved char. Standard HTTP routing decodes path segments;
the dispatcher should too.

Fix: map decodeURIComponent over each segment (after split, so an encoded
"%2F" stays as one segment rather than being split into two), wrapped in
a try/catch so malformed encoding (e.g. lone "%") returns [] → 404. Two
new dispatcher tests pin both behaviors.

(2) Misleading cron verification in USER_ACTIONS.md. The previous
guidance suggested running the cron endpoints manually with CRON_SECRET
"to verify routing." That is unsafe: nudge sends real notifications and
auto-expire actually expires modules. Running them against production
fires real user-visible side effects.

Replace with side-effect-free verification: curl without Authorization,
expect 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}. That response proves the dispatcher
matched the cron route and reached the handler's auth check, with no
notifications sent and no modules expired. The doc lists all three
distinguishable outcomes (401 = pass; sancio 404 = dispatch regression;
HTML 404 = Vercel routing broken) so the user can read the failure mode
off the response alone.

Tags: bugfix, sancio, dispatcher, testing, documentation, cron, safety
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: percent-decode URL segments; correct cron verification guidance

commits.subject: docs(sancio): pin rename to Vercel build/edge layer; add cron verification curls
commits.body:
Tightening pass on the prior fix:

(1) Attribution: the catch-all key rename (slug → ...slug) does not come
from @vercel/node — that package only parses req.query from the URL it
receives. The rename is in Vercel's build/edge routing layer, which
rewrites incoming URLs onto the catch-all by appending a query string
whose key is the literal bracketed name from the filename. AI_MISTAKES
and the dispatcher header now name the correct subsystem; the lesson
("route from req.url, not req.query") is unchanged. Investigation
against @vercel/node's changelog and routing source did not pin the
rename to a specific commit, but the rename is observable end-to-end
via req.url, which is what the fix relies on.

(2) Cron verification: the cron endpoints (/api/sancio/cron/nudge,
/auto-expire) share the dispatcher and were also broken, but Vercel
only invokes them on schedule (08:00 / 09:00 UTC). USER_ACTIONS.md now
includes manual verification curls using CRON_SECRET so the user can
confirm cron routing without waiting for the next scheduled run. Both
handlers are idempotent (no-op on modules not yet stale/expired), so
running them outside the schedule is safe.

Tags: documentation, sancio, vercel, cron
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: pin rename to Vercel build/edge layer; add cron verification curls

commits.subject: fix(sancio): route from req.url, not req.query, to survive Vercel param-key renames
commits.body:
@vercel/node silently renamed the catch-all key from `slug` to `...slug`
(literal three-dot prefix from the [...slug].ts filename), making
req.query.slug undefined for every Sancio request. Production logs
showed `url: '/api/sancio/projects?...slug=projects'` with
`rawSlug: undefined` — the value lived at req.query["...slug"]. Every
sancio endpoint 404ed across all consuming frontends; CI was green
because the dispatcher tests drove req.query.slug directly.

Switch routing to read req.url: strip the /api/sancio prefix, split the
remainder on /, drop empties. req.url is HTTP-level state Vercel cannot
rename out from under us. Drop the rawSlug field from the fallthrough
log; add queryKeys (key names only, not values, to avoid leaking auth
tokens or PII) so a future framework rename is immediately visible in
Vercel logs.

Rewrite dispatcher tests to drive req.url shaped like production
(including the `...slug=` rewrite query string) instead of synthetic
req.query.slug. Add a regression-guard test asserting that
req.query.slug alone does NOT dispatch — pinning the new contract so a
future "fallback to query" change surfaces here.

Update docs/AI_MISTAKES.md and CLAUDE.md with the lesson: for Vercel
catch-all routes, route from req.url, not req.query.

Tags: bugfix, sancio, vercel, dispatcher, testing, documentation
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 5
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
commits.title: route from req.url, not req.query, to survive Vercel param-key renames

commits.body:
The dispatcher 404 bug was invisible to TS and the test suite because
the unsafe cast told both that the runtime contract matched the type.
Future AI work on framework-typed query params should normalize at the
boundary, never `as`-cast away union arms.

Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal

Semver: patch

commits.title: record `as string[]` cast as root cause pattern

commits.subject: fix(sancio): unblock single-segment routes; harden multi-segment routing
commits.body:
Root cause: api/sancio/[...path].ts read `req.query.path as string[]`. The
cast lied — Vercel's runtime delivers the catch-all param as a bare string
for single-segment URLs, so segments became "projects" instead of
["projects"], no segments[0] branch matched, and every single-segment
sancio request fell through to the explicit 404. The per-handler tests
import resource handlers directly and bypass dispatch, so this never
failed a test.

Changes:
- Add normalizeSegments(raw) at the top of the dispatcher: handles
  string[] (documented shape), bare string (split on / and filter
  Boolean — covers single AND multi-segment string forms), and undefined.
- Add a `console.error("[sancio] fallthrough", { url, method, rawSlug,
  segments })` line before the final 404 so any future regression
  self-diagnoses in one Vercel-logs query.
- vercel.json: drop "framework": "vite". Vite is auto-detected from
  vite.config.ts; declaring it explicitly may invoke SPA-aware behavior
  that interferes with file-based API routing on the platform.
- Rename api/sancio/[...path].ts -> [...slug].ts (via git mv) and read
  req.query.slug. `path` is generic and overlaps with Node/URL
  conventions; `slug` rules out a Vercel-reserved query-key collision
  and reads more clearly. Doc references in CLAUDE.md and API.md updated.

Test coverage:
- New api/__tests__/sancio/dispatcher.test.ts (45 tests). Mocks every
  lib/routes/sancio/* module with named "probe" functions that record
  their name + post-mutation req.query, then drives every documented
  route shape through the dispatcher. Coverage:
    * Input shape normalization: string[], bare string, slash-separated
      string, leading/trailing slashes, missing slug, empty array.
    * Route table: all 26 dispatch branches, asserting both the right
      probe ran AND req.query.id was set for by-id routes.
    * 404 fallthrough: 11 unknown-path cases plus a console.error
      diagnostic assertion.
    * CORS short-circuit: dispatcher returns early without invoking any
      handler when cors() returns true.

Operational follow-up:
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: post-deploy verification curl set + Vercel
  support escalation procedure (capture diverging x-vercel-id values
  from a working single-segment call and a failing multi-segment call)
  in case the platform 404 on multi-segment routes persists despite
  the framework + rename changes.

Tags: sancio, routing, vercel, bugfix, testing, dispatcher
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sun-sea-o-integration
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KeVVr1G1A7zVa4Yi2NTmfT
commits.title: unblock single-segment routes; harden multi-segment routing

commits.subject: chore: fresh-eyes branch polish after cold audit
commits.body:
Six small findings from the cold-review pass, none blocking:

- CLAUDE.md: the Architecture line for lib/auth.ts now mentions the
  temporary sancio bypass so a fresh reader sees it before hitting AI Notes.
- lib/cors.ts: brief inline comment explaining why X-Dev-User-Id /
  X-Dev-User-Email sit in the preflight allowlist and pointing at the
  removal path.
- api/__tests__/auth.test.ts: save and restore process.env around each
  test instead of leaving SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_ANON_KEY set after the
  suite runs. Vitest isolates workers so the risk was low, but hardening.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: expanded the removal-path step 3 run-on sentence
  into sub-bullets so flip-day operator can tick each off.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: dropped point-in-time facts that silently rot
  ("19 commits ahead," "95 tests passing") in favour of durable wording.
- src/App.tsx: typed the fetch-catch err as unknown and narrowed via
  `instanceof Error` before reading .name. Promise.catch's signature types
  the param as any so strict mode didn't flag the implicit any, but the
  explicit unknown + narrowing reads clearer and matches the pattern the
  rest of the repo uses for untrusted values.

Flagged but intentionally not fixed (documented in the cold pass):
- Preflight advertises dev headers to all allowed origins, not just
  sun-sea-o. Per-origin preflight logic adds code for zero functional
  benefit — no opt-in means no bypass regardless of what the preflight
  advertises.
- lib/auth.ts accepts any non-empty string as x-dev-user-id without UUID
  validation. Code is slated for deletion; downstream DB UUID parse
  failures are self-limiting for a dev tool.

commits.subject: fix(cors): allowlist X-Dev-User-Id and X-Dev-User-Email on preflight
commits.body:
Without this, browsers strip both headers from sun-sea-o's preflighted
sancio requests and the bypass silently 401s on every call. The surface
pass on the auth-bypass work caught it — unit tests cover the function
behavior but never the browser preflight path.

Verified sun-sea-o/src/api/client.ts sends exactly `X-Dev-User-Id` +
`X-Dev-User-Email` (PascalCase hyphenated) with the BYPASS_USER UUID
00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001 — matches what lib/auth.ts reads
and what the test data uses.

Updated the health.test.ts preflight assertion to match the new header
list. Other CORS tests unchanged.

commits.subject: feat(auth): drop SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS env var gate, opt-in arg alone controls bypass
commits.body:
The env gate was added as defense-in-depth plus a kill-switch. In practice:
- The kill-switch requires a redeploy anyway (Vercel env change is not instant
  without one), so it has no speed advantage over a code change.
- The defense-in-depth only helps if the wrong call site gets `allowDevBypass:
  true` merged — unlikely given review, and the mistake is still caught by the
  "non-sancio routes unaffected" test.
- The activation friction is real: every fresh Vercel deploy / preview env /
  project clone needs a manual env var set, and a single misconfigured env
  produces silent 401s on every sancio call from sun-sea-o.

This project is pre-first-release with a plan to delete the bypass code
entirely before real auth ships. The env gate buys little for that timeline.
Removing it so the deployed sun-sea-o works end-to-end from the moment the
backend deploy lands.

Changes:
- lib/auth.ts: drop env check, update JSDoc to describe single-gate design
  and the now-simplified removal procedure.
- api/__tests__/auth.test.ts: drop two env-gate tests (env unset, non-"true"
  env value) and update titles on remaining 6 tests.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: drop the Vercel env var activation entry entirely.
  Replace with a follow-up action for sun-sea-o's USER_ACTIONS.md (which
  currently references the env var as "the second gate" and is now stale).
  Simplify the removal path — no env var to remove, just the code.

The 25 sancio call sites still pass `{ allowDevBypass: true }` — no change
needed there.

Tags: auth, sancio, dev-bypass, simplification
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sun-sea-o-integration
Semver: patch
commits.title: drop SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS env var gate, opt-in arg alone controls bypass

commits.subject: feat(auth): sancio-scoped dev auth bypass for sun-sea-o AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED
commits.body:
Closes the backend half of the sun-sea-o dev auth bypass. When the frontend
ships with AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED=true it sends X-Dev-User-Id + X-Dev-User-Email
instead of Authorization: Bearer, and every sancio call 401s until the
backend also accepts those headers.

Design:
- Two-gate: opt-in arg `{ allowDevBypass: true }` AND env `SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS=true`.
  Either alone has no effect, so a forgotten opt-in committed to main cannot
  become a production backdoor without the env var, and an accidentally-set
  env var cannot affect non-sancio routes.
- Call-site opt-in instead of URL-prefix check: scoping is explicit in code
  review, doesn't couple auth to routing, and fails closed for any new sancio
  route added without the opt-in.
- X-Dev-User-Email is ignored server-side — UUID is the identity.
- All 25 sancio call sites (across 10 route files) pass allowDevBypass:true.
  Non-sancio call sites (api/forms/*, api/submissions/*) unchanged — still
  require JWT regardless of env state.

Tests cover the full matrix: env+opt-in+header → bypass; env+opt-in+no header
→ JWT fallthrough; env+no opt-in+header → 401 (non-sancio case); no env+opt-in
+header → 401 (env gate); non-"true" env values → 401; empty header → 401;
valid JWT passthrough unchanged; invalid JWT still 401 even with bypass env on.

USER_ACTIONS.md updated with the Vercel env var step and the full removal
path for first real release (both sides must flip together).

Tags: auth, sancio, dev-bypass, sun-sea-o
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: added
Epic: sun-sea-o-integration
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(sancio): standardize all API request/response fields to snake_case
commits.body:
Renames 10 camelCase request body fields and 1 response field across 6
Sancio endpoints to snake_case, matching the existing response convention
(raw Supabase rows). This eliminates the naming inconsistency where
validated fields (via validation.ts) used snake_case but inline-validated
fields used camelCase.

Fields renamed:
- projectId → project_id (invite, verify-passphrase req + response)
- counterpartyEmail → counterparty_email (invite)
- passphraseLink → passphrase_link (invite)
- moduleIds → module_ids (split, batch-ready)
- newTitle → new_title (split)
- moduleOrder → module_order (sequence)
- responseContent → response_content (resolve proposal)
- agreementId → agreement_id (create verification)

BREAKING: sun-sea-o frontend must be updated to send snake_case field
names. Deploy backend first, then frontend immediately after.

Tags: api, sancio, naming, consistency

commits.subject: Merge pull request #20 from devmade-ai/claude/align-field-conditions-aGMoa

commits.title: Merge pull request #20 from devmade-ai/claude/align-field-conditions-aGMoa

commits.subject: fix(forms): align condition operator behavior and validation with frontend
commits.body:
The previous commit aligned structural types but operator behavior still diverged
from four-ems, risking silent data loss when backend condition evaluation produced
different visibility results than the frontend.

Conditions: equals/not_equals now normalize to trimmed strings and sort arrays
(checkbox groups), contains/not_contains are case-insensitive, greater_than/
less_than use Number() coercion (builder stores values as strings), is_empty/
is_not_empty trim strings and handle booleans/numbers explicitly.

Validation: phone regex matches frontend (no dots/slashes, max 20 chars), date
fields enforce min/max bounds, text regex has ReDoS protection (isSafeRegex +
1000-char cap).

Tags: forms, conditions, validation, security, alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: field-conditions-alignment
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JqVFZ316cyaysF7G984H7C
commits.title: align condition operator behavior and validation with frontend

commits.subject: fix(forms): align field types and condition logic with frontend
commits.body:
The frontend (four-ems) and backend had diverged on field types and
condition evaluation. checkbox_group submissions were unvalidated (fell
through to default), condition model used different shapes (fieldId vs
field_id, targetFieldId vs per-field conditions), and type definitions
included backend-only types with no frontend components.

Changes:
- FieldType: removed url/file/scale/multi_select, added checkbox_group/heading/hidden
- Condition: fieldId → field_id, value accepts string[], added in/not_in operators
- ConditionGroup: targetFieldId → target_id + target_type + action (show/hide)
- FormField: added frontend rendering-hint properties (layout, min/max_selections, etc.)
- Validation: checkbox_group with min/max selections, separate rating from number, removed dead cases
- Conditions: per-field conditions, page-level conditions, action-aware evaluation

Tags: forms, validation, conditions, types, alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: four-ems-forms
Semver: minor

commits.subject: feat(sancio): add missing validation fields, new endpoints, and unscoped GETs
commits.body:
Frontend (sun-sea-o) has migrated from direct Supabase to the REST API.
This implements the remaining gaps so affected features stop failing at runtime.

1. Validation gaps (existing endpoints):
   - validateModuleUpdate: add intent, summary_does, summary_when, summary_consequence
   - validateNoteInput: add sender_tone
   - validateProposalInput: add sender_tone

2. New endpoints:
   - POST /projects/:id/accept-handshake — counterparty handshake acceptance
   - POST /modules/:id/move — move module up/down (swap positions)
   - POST /modules/batch-ready — batch toggle ready-to-sign

3. Unscoped GET endpoints (optional parent ID params):
   - GET /agreements — optional projectId
   - GET /modules — optional agreementId
   - GET /notes — optional agreementId
   - GET /proposals — optional moduleId
   - GET /pdf-verifications — optional agreementId/code

17 new validation tests added. All 89 tests pass. Typecheck clean.

The auto-advance rule let the AI skip through all triggers in one
message when findings were empty. This defeats the purpose of the
sequential sweep. Now the AI must always stop and wait for the user
after each trigger, even with no findings.

commits.subject: feat(cors): replace wildcard with dynamic origin allowlist
commits.body:
Created lib/cors.ts with an allowlist of approved frontend origins
(devmade-ai.github.io, four-ems.vercel.app). Each handler calls cors()
which checks the request Origin against the allowlist and sets
Access-Control-Allow-Origin to the matched origin with Vary: Origin.
Unrecognized origins get no CORS header — the browser blocks the request.

Removed Access-Control-Allow-Origin from vercel.json (other CORS headers
remain static). Updated all 11 API handlers to use cors() instead of
inline OPTIONS checks.

Tags: cors, security, api, config
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: four-ems
Semver: minor

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BqPM5pcAcdzxTW9FDe4poV
commits.title: replace wildcard with dynamic origin allowlist

commits.subject: fix(cors): switch Access-Control-Allow-Origin to wildcard
commits.body:
Vercel's static headers in vercel.json only accept a single string value,
so we can't list multiple origins. Since auth is purely token-based
(Authorization: Bearer JWT) with no cookies, wildcard (*) is safe and
allows all frontend consumers (devmade-ai.github.io, four-ems.vercel.app,
and any future frontends) without per-origin config.

Also removed the 403 "Origin not allowed" response from openapi.yaml and
API.md since no origin will be rejected with wildcard CORS.

Tags: cors, config, docs
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: four-ems
Semver: patch

commits.title: switch Access-Control-Allow-Origin to wildcard

commits.subject: refactor(sancio): consolidate 23 endpoints into catch-all router
commits.body:
Vercel Hobby plan limits deployments to 12 serverless functions.
The project had 33 (10 non-sancio + 23 sancio), causing deploy failure.

Consolidation approach:
- Created api/sancio/[...path].ts as single catch-all router
- Moved handler logic to lib/routes/sancio/ grouped by resource (11 files)
- Router only does URL path dispatch; handlers are self-contained
- Updated test imports to point to new handler locations
- All 70 tests pass, typecheck clean

Result: 10 non-sancio + 1 sancio catch-all = 11 functions (under limit)

Alternatives considered:
- Consolidate forms too: rejected — unnecessary, already under limit
- Single monolithic handler: rejected — poor maintainability
- _handlers convention: rejected — Vercel ignores _ prefix but less clear

Tags: refactor, vercel, deployment, sancio
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio
Semver: patch

commits.title: consolidate 23 endpoints into catch-all router

commits.subject: Refactor: workspace_id → user_id, align with synctone Supabase auth
commits.body:
Replace workspace_id with user_id across the entire forms backend to
match the existing synctone Supabase project which uses anonymous auth
via auth.uid().

Changes:
- Migration: forms.user_id references auth.users(id), RLS policies use
  auth.uid() instead of current_setting('app.workspace_id')
- Auth middleware: returns user.id directly from JWT instead of looking
  up workspace_id in app_metadata
- All API endpoints: filter by user_id instead of workspace_id
- Types: Form interface uses user_id

Move Supabase client creation from top-level (module load time) to lazy
getSupabase() function so missing env vars don't crash unrelated endpoints.
Move auth env var reads inside the request handler for the same reason.
Existing /api/health and /api/send-interest endpoints are unaffected.

Implement the full backend for the Four Ems form builder:

  indexes, and auto-update trigger

  (Supabase anonymous auth), field condition evaluation, and submission
  validation (type checks, required fields, conditional visibility)

  update submission status

commits.subject: Fix send-interest crash: add .js extension to ESM import of lib/constants
commits.body:
The extensionless import from "../lib/constants" worked in dev/test (Vite/Vitest
handle resolution) but failed at Vercel's Node.js ESM runtime which requires
explicit .js extensions for relative imports. This caused every request to
/api/send-interest to crash before any code ran—including CORS preflight—which
the mobile frontend diagnosed as a CORS failure.

commits.subject: Fix API 404s by switching from legacy builds to zero-config Vercel
commits.body:
The legacy `builds` property in vercel.json combined with api/package.json
caused Vercel to treat the api/ directory as a single function unit (only
exposing index.ts), making /api/health and /api/send-interest return 404.

- Remove `builds` array to use Vercel's modern auto-detection pipeline
- Add `framework: null` to prevent framework auto-detection
- Delete api/package.json (redundant — root package.json already provides
  "type": "module" and nodemailer dependency)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UaGdKLgaAMkoTv4G1ga8sc

as CommonJS and fail with "SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'export'".

commits.subject: Add api/package.json for Vercel function-level dependency install
commits.body:
The @vercel/node builder runs npm install inside api/ for each
function. Without a package.json there, the install fails with ENOENT.
Declare nodemailer (the only runtime dep) so the builder can install it.

commits.subject: Use builds config to bypass static-site pipeline entirely
commits.body:
The zero-config pipeline assumes a static site and demands an output
directory. Previous attempts to satisfy it (buildCommand: "", mkdir,
placeholder files) kept hitting the next check. Switch to the builds
allowlist model: only api/*.ts files are built via @vercel/node, no
output directory needed. Root npm install still runs for dependencies.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Use builds config to bypass static-site pipeline entirely

commits.subject: Fix build: output directory must be non-empty
commits.body:
Vercel rejects an empty output directory. Write a placeholder file so
the static output check passes and function deployment proceeds.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Fix build: output directory must be non-empty

commits.subject: Fix build: use mkdir for output dir instead of skipping build
commits.body:
buildCommand: "" skips the entire install+build pipeline, so Vercel
tries a per-function npm install in api/ which fails (no package.json
there). Instead, let the normal pipeline run and create an empty
public/ at build time to satisfy the output directory check.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Fix build: use mkdir for output dir instead of skipping build

commits.subject: Move constants.ts out of api/ to prevent stray Vercel endpoint
commits.body:
Vercel treats every file in api/ as a serverless function. constants.ts
is a helper module (no handler), so it was being exposed as a broken
/api/constants endpoint. Move it to lib/ where Vercel won't touch it.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Move constants.ts out of api/ to prevent stray Vercel endpoint

commits.subject: Configure Vercel for API-only deployment, remove unused favicons
commits.body:
This is a pure serverless API with no frontend — favicons were never
referenced by any code. Instead of keeping dead static files to satisfy
Vercel's output directory check, configure vercel.json properly:
- "framework": null declares no frontend framework
- "buildCommand": "" skips the build step entirely, so Vercel never
  looks for an output directory

Replaces the workaround from 7fc7936 that moved favicons to public/.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Configure Vercel for API-only deployment, remove unused favicons

commits.subject: Move favicons to public/ to fix Vercel build error
commits.body:
Vercel expects a "public" output directory for non-framework projects.
The no-op build command (true) doesn't create one, causing the build
to fail with "No Output Directory named public found". Moving favicons
into public/ ensures the directory exists and static files are served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Move favicons to public/ to fix Vercel build error

commits.subject: Fix Vercel 404s by restructuring project to deploy from repo root
commits.body:
Vercel's GitHub integration uses the repo root as the project root,
but all config and function files were nested inside an api/ subdirectory.
This caused vercel.json to not be found and serverless functions to map
to /api/api/* instead of /api/*, resulting in 404s on all endpoints.

Flatten the structure: move config files (vercel.json, package.json,
tsconfig.json, etc.) to repo root, move serverless functions from
api/api/ to api/, and place favicons at repo root for static serving.
Update documentation paths accordingly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018GdxVNVycPS8x1KTytw3hi
commits.title: Fix Vercel 404s by restructuring project to deploy from repo root

commits.subject: Fix serverless function deployment by removing vercel devDep and adding build script
commits.body:
Two issues were preventing successful Vercel builds:

1. `vercel` in devDependencies caused Vercel to warn "will be
   ignored" and strip it before npm install, creating a mismatch
   with the lockfile. Removed it since the Vercel CLI is provided
   by the platform.

2. No `build` script existed — if the Vercel project has a build
   command configured (even the default `npm run build`), it would
   fail. Added a no-op build script since serverless functions
   don't need a build step (Vercel compiles TypeScript automatically).

https://claude.ai/code/session_017jorRZB6ANxXDQhyH1Xdom
commits.title: Fix serverless function deployment by removing vercel devDep and adding build script

commits.subject: Move favicons to project root for Vercel static file serving
commits.body:
Vercel only auto-serves files from public/ for framework projects
(Next.js, etc.). For plain serverless projects, static files must
be at the project root. Since the Vercel root directory is set to
api/, move favicon.ico and favicon.png from api/public/ to api/.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017jorRZB6ANxXDQhyH1Xdom
commits.title: Move favicons to project root for Vercel static file serving

commits.subject: Fix Vercel 404s by restructuring project to standard layout
commits.body:
All routes (/, /favicon.ico, /favicon.png, /api/*) returned 404
because the Vercel project files were nested inside an api/
subdirectory. Vercel's default root directory is the repo root,
so it couldn't find vercel.json, the serverless functions, or
static assets.

Restructure to the standard Vercel layout:
- vercel.json, package.json, tsconfig.json at repo root
- api/ contains serverless function handlers
- public/ contains static assets (favicon.ico, favicon.png)

commits.subject: Add interest notification API and frontend form
commits.body:
Set up Vercel serverless functions for an interest/contact notification
system. The send-interest endpoint validates input (name, email, message),
enforces CORS origin restrictions, includes honeypot spam protection, and
sends emails via SMTP using nodemailer. A health endpoint is included for
deployment verification. The frontend InterestForm React component handles
form state, submission, and gracefully degrades when the API URL is not
configured.

validates input (name, email, message) with length limits and format
checks, enforces CORS via ALLOWED_ORIGINS, includes honeypot spam
detection, escapes HTML in email body, and sends via nodemailer/SMTP.
Also adds a /api/health endpoint and project scaffolding (tsconfig,
vercel.json, .env.example).
